items.title: 'Sacred' Coorong wetlands bracing for impact of bird flu

children.content: South Australia's Coorong is a sanctuary for birds and marine life but it is increasingly under threat with the arrival of bird flu in Australia the latest in a rolling barrage of crises.

articles.title: First native bird found with H5 bird flu, marking 'devastating' new reality

children.content: CSIRO lab testing has confirmed the detection of H5 bird flu in a greater crested tern — the first time the strain has been detected in an Australian seabird, after previous confirmed cases involved migratory birds from the sub-Antarctic region.

image.altText: A greater crested tern on South Africa's western cape.

gallery.altText: Drone picture of the Coorong, showing lagoons and sand dunes.
Two black swams swim on a lake in the Coorong.

thumbnail.altText: Drone picture of the Coorong, showing lagoons and sand dunes.
Two black swams swim on a lake in the Coorong.

items.title: One dead after suspected carbon monoxide poisoning in Mount Gambier

children.content: A 55-year-old has died and three other men have been taken to hospital after a case of suspected carbon monoxide poisoning at a Mount Gambier home in South Australia's south-east.

image.altText: Emergency services vehicles in Mount Gambier.

items.title: Workers waiting a year for entitlements from GFG may never be paid

children.content: Jun Samonte has spent 14 years working for the same Whyalla business. He now fears he will never receive the $53,000 he is owed in entitlements, despite already waiting a year.

image.altText: A man in a yellow high vis shirt and a beany both of which say Ferretti International.

items.title: Broken Hill's long legacy of lead exposure

children.content: Baylee Stokes lives with the health impacts of being exposed to lead as a child. Now, she's considering moving away from her hometown to protect her own children.

gallery.altText: A mother with dyed red hair on a chair with a toddler with red hair on the lap

thumbnail.altText: A mother with dyed red hair on a chair with a toddler with red hair on the lap

items.title: Cats star Cameron taken to hospital, as Blues battle Hawks in MCG downpour

children.content: Jeremy Cameron has been taken to hospital after a huge clash with Clayton Oliver, as the AFL's two late games get underway. Follow live.

image.altText: A Geelong AFL player sits in a wheelchair with medical staff as he waits to go to hospital.

items.title: Claim woman's death caused by Telstra outage is 'incorrect', SA Police say

children.content: South Australia's police chief says there is "nothing to suggest" the death of a person in a regional hospital was caused by any failure to connect to Triple Zero (000) during the Telstra outage, following an investigation into the matter.

image.altText: Close up of mobile phone with SOS message due to Telstra outage

items.title: Adelaide Crows player in collision involving pedestrian

children.content: Police are investigating the collision which took place on Port Road at Woodville on Friday morning.

image.altText: crows players Josh worrell, text walker and others walking off field

items.title: Farmers search for ways to break free from controversial herbicide

children.content: Australia buys most of its paraquat from China, but is investigating ways to become less reliant on the chemical.

image.altText: A man wearing a cap, crouching in a paddock of wheat.

items.title: Pedestrians 'lucky to be alive' after truck loses load on highway

children.content: Footage shows two 15-tonne concrete slabs colliding with parked cars when the incident happened north of Adelaide.

image.altText: Road camera shows truck losing its load on the North-South Motorway near Bolivar.

gallery.altText: Large concrete slabs fall from a moving truck on a motorway

thumbnail.altText: Large concrete slabs fall from a moving truck on a motorway

items.title: This Place: Redbank Ngarindjerri

children.content: A grandfather and daughter in South Australia's Riverland are on a journey to revive culture, connection to country and Ngarrindjeri language for the next generation.
As part of NAIDOC Week the ABC is looking at Indigenous peoples' connection to country, and what makes it so special.
NAIDOC Week is marking 50 years of Deadly and runs from July 5 until July 12.

image.altText: Amn Aboriginal elder describes his story about this place by the river.

gallery.altText: Amn Aboriginal elder describes his story about this place by the river.

thumbnail.altText: Amn Aboriginal elder describes his story about this place by the river.

items.title: Getting guernseys reverses fortunes of one of the most remote footy clubs

children.content: The Fregon Bulldogs are among the nation's most remote Australian Rules football clubs, and for several seasons were forced to play in casual clothes. But the club has secured new guernseys — and the change in uniform has led to a change in form.

image.altText: The new design of the guernsey of the Fregon Bulldogs football club.

items.title: No parking signs turfed into creek at hospital redevelopment site

children.content: Residents say the streets surrounding a Mount Barker hospital construction site are inundated with workers' vehicles, and some have rejected temporary "no parking" signs.

image.altText: no parking sign on the ground next to colourful fence

items.title: Police release video of killer Bradley Murdoch filmed weeks before his death

children.content: In never-before-seen footage of police questioning Bradley John Murdoch in his final weeks, the notorious murderer repeatedly tells officers he "knows nothing" about the location of Peter Falconio's remains.

image.altText: Body-worn vision from a police officer shows a tall, older man speaking to two people and pointing his finger.

gallery.altText: Body-worn vision from a police officer shows a tall, older man speaking to two people and pointing his finger.

thumbnail.altText: Body-worn vision from a police officer shows a tall, older man speaking to two people and pointing his finger.

items.title: Court case against chair of Indian steelmaker won't affect Whyalla sale, SA government says

children.content: The chairperson of one of the bidders for the stricken Whyalla steelworks has been summonsed to appear before an Indian court, but the government says its confident the sale process will maintain integrity.

image.altText: A factory and furnace against the backdrop of sunset and a lake in the distance

items.title: Hikers warned to take 'gnarly' peri-urban parks seriously after rescue

children.content: Three teenagers were rescued from Onkaparinga River National Park unscathed on Sunday, showing how quickly hikers can come unstuck on spectacular walking trails despite their proximity to Adelaide.

image.altText: Young man pulls a face at a plaque with a view of gorge in the background

items.title: 'Restraining order needed': The fight between Trump and former ally Meloni

children.content: Falling out with Donald Trump is not a situation most world leaders want to find themselves in, but for Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni, a spat with the US president could end up playing well for her.

articles.title: Trump wanted NATO to be about him. Instead, it was about everyone else

children.content: Trump demanded to be the centre of attention. But the more significant news out of NATO was really about everyone else.

image.altText: president trump's face close up - looking displease

articles.title: Iran denies requesting US talks, as Trump maintains ceasefire is 'over'

children.content: Qatari negotiators meet with officials in Iran to seek de-escalation, as Iran denies requesting talks with the United States following a statement from US President Donald Trump.

image.altText: Donald Trump speaks to the media at a conference

articles.title: England captain Kane confirms 'surreal' golf experience with Trump

children.content: England's captain Harry Kane opens up about his 18-hole golf experience with the US President on a previous visit to Palm Beach, Florida.

items.title: Suspect in former UK MP's murder investigation released

children.content: UK police say the man has been released from custody and is "no longer part of the investigation" into Ann Widdecombe's death.

image.altText: A close up of an older woman in red with a microphone in hand.

items.title: France blows Wallabies away in Nations Championship Test

children.content: France scores it fourth straight win over the Wallabies, triumphing 42-26 in the Nations Championship Test in Brisbane. Follow live.

image.altText: Fraser McReight attmepts to palm off a French opponent.

items.title: That's life: Derryn Hinch's most memorable media moments

children.content: A man of colourful contradictions, Derryn Hinch was a larger-than-life fixture on Australian radio and television over five decades who died on Friday morning at the age of 82.

articles.title: Hinch revealed a scenario that seemed too crazy to be true — but he was right

children.content: Derryn Hinch was not a hero to every survivor of sexual abuse, but in the decades when they lacked allies, he was their fiercest public advocate.

image.altText: A man with a beard and glasses, photographed from below

gallery.altText: Derryn Hinch on the TV show 'Hinch'

thumbnail.altText: Derryn Hinch on the TV show 'Hinch'

items.title: Footballer Nathan Fitzgerald farewelled in emotional ceremony

children.content: The stands were full at Epping Recreation Reserve as teammates, family and friends of footballer Nathan Fitzgerald gathered for a memorial, one week after his fatal accident.

image.altText: Nathan Fitzgerald

items.title: Car maker admits selling wrong year vehicles to 1,200 people

children.content: More than 1,200 customers who bought electric and hybrid cars from Chinese maker BYD will be offered a full refund after the company admitted to selling them vehicles from the wrong year.

image.altText: A man leaning next to his vehicle with his elbow on the hood, his reflection in the side window of the car.

items.title: England and Norway fans dash to Miami for World Cup spring break

children.content: As England and Norway prepare to face off in their World Cup quarter-final, their fans are lapping up being in one of the party capitals of the world, making every moment count.

articles.title: World Cup contends with heat in test case for sport

children.content: A 38-degree Celsius evening match has raised fresh questions about how football manages heat in a warming world.

image.altText: Several Norwegian soccer players looking very hot and sweaty

articles.title: Quick hits: Spain's new clutch king and a Colombian death threat scare

children.content: Spain's new clutch king saves the day, a record-breaking streak is finally broken and a Colombian midfielder receives death threats. Here are today's World Cup quick hits.

items.title: The 99-year-old Victorian mechanic on the verge of a world record

children.content: Ray Gloster has been a mechanic for 83 years and counting, and at 99, he might be on the verge of a world record.

children.content: Residents of the Indigenous community of Beagle Bay say they feel forgotten by their landlord: the Western Australian government.

items.title: Man tied up, house burnt down as thieves steal 9 guns, car

children.content: Emergency services called to a house fire at Tasmania's Coles Bay find the home owner tied up and his home torched after thieves made off with nine guns and his car.

image.altText: Rifles on display at Tasmanian firearms seller.

items.title: Australian scientist ignites war over the world's longest lives

children.content: When an Australian academic began questioning the science behind "Blue Zones", he sparked a bitter battle in the field of longevity research.

items.title: How Jessica Mauboy is marking NAIDOC Week with her daughter

children.content: From singing to her daughter to laughing with her aunties, Jessica Mauboy is all about family. This NAIDOC Week, the star is sharing the music, culture and community she grew up with in a whole new way.

articles.title: Handmade quilts babies 'will have for life' wrap them in culture

children.content: Beautiful Indigenous-themed quilts have been made by a group of women for a NAIDOC Week baby celebration on Worimi country, linking cultures and generations.

image.altText: An Indigenous baby smiling in his mum's arms, with a brightly coloured patchwork quilt.

articles.title: Aboriginal artist recreating 'oldest living weaving practice' with glass

children.content: Lower Southern Arrernte woman Jenni Kemarre Martiniello creates glass artworks by combining a centuries-old Italian glassmaking technique with millennia-old Indigenous weaving practices.

image.altText: A person's hands working on a woven-patterned glass sculpture with a blowtorch.

articles.title: Indigenous-led whale tour shares thousands of years of observations and knowledge

children.content: A new Indigenous-led whale-watching cruise is aiming to give an intimate insight into First Nations people's deep historical connection with the majestic mammals.

image.altText: Two large whales breach over the water and are captured mid air.
